get.summary:
Returns a httpStream of the file contents of the specified Safeguard Appliance backup. The name is supplied
        at the user end and the standard backup extension is '.sgb'. Set vmCompatible to true if you wish to download a backup
        from hardware for subsequent restore on a virtual appliance.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique id of the Safeguard backup file to download.

parameters.name: vmCompatible
parameters.description:
If downloading a Safeguard backup file from a physical Safeguard hardware appliance, as
        opposed to a virtual machine instance of Safeguard, then you can specify whether the backup can be applied/restored
        onto a virtual machine. By default, a Safeguard backup file cannot be applied/restored onto a Safeguard virtual machine
        instance.

400.description:
70003 - The id field must be a valid GUID value.
            
60287 - The backup cannot be downloaded until it has completed.
            
60916 - VM compatible download requires the selected backup was generated on this appliance.
            
60917 - VM compatible download requires the selected backup has additional protection.

403.description:
Must have ApplianceAdmin, OperationsAdmin, SystemAuditor or Auditor permission.
            
60385 - This operation is not supported for this platform type.
            
60915 - VM compatible download requires this appliance has VM Compatible Backup Authorization.

post.summary: Starts a new backup restore operation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of backup.

get.summary: Gets all of the appliance's settings.
get.description:
Setting Name Description
Enable Scheduled Task Debugging For diagnostics of platform tasks, like Change Password, whether or not to include logging/debugging information while executing the task.
Minimum Process Log Level For diagnostics of platform tasks, like Change Password, whether or not to include more or less logging/debugging information on this specific appliance. Use in conjunction with the Enable Scheduled Task Debugging setting.
Backup Retention Number The total number of backup files that will be stored locally on this appliance. Once the maximum is reached, the oldest stored backup file will be deleted as new files are created.
Max Task Threads For tuning the workload distribution of this appliance within the cluster.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter results. Available operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, contains, ieq, icontains, sw, isw, ew, iew, in [ {item1}, {item2}, etc], (). Use \ to escape quotes, asterisks and backslashes in strings.

parameters.description: The size of a page of data.

get.summary: Gets a support bundle (streamed .zip folder) reflecting the current product state.

parameters.name: includeEventLogs
parameters.description: Specify whether or not to include operating system event logs. This takes much longer to generate the support bundle.

parameters.name: includeSessionLogs
parameters.description: (Deprecated) This parameter has no effect.

parameters.name: logRetentionDays
parameters.description: Log retention specified in days. Safeguard retains up to 30 days of logs. Use this parameter to limit the number of logs returned. 0 returns today's logs, 1 includes yesterday's logs and so on. This can greatly reduce the size of the support bundle for triage scenarios.

403.description: Must have ApplianceAdmin, SystemAuditor or Auditor permission.

429.description: Only one Support Bundle can be requested at a time.

get.summary: Gets the time on the appliance.

put.summary:
Updates the time on the appliance. You may experience some date issues, including problems with HTTP date headers,
if the system time is changed from a future date to a date before that
future date. This issue can be fixed by rebooting the appliance.

403.description: Must have ApplianceAdmin or OperationsAdmin permission.

get.summary: Gets the version of the software on the appliance.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to include in entity output. Prepend list with '-' to exclude from output.

ServicePort.description:
Port that the A2A service will listen on. By default, it is set to port 443. Otherwise it must be an ephemeral port
greater than or equal to 1024.

A2AServiceConfiguration.description: Configuration information for the Application to Application service.

Identity.description: Unique hardware-specific identity of this appliance (Read-only).

Name.description:
Name of this appliance
If HostDnsSuffix is set, when combined with Name to produce a DNS name, the DNS Name must be less than 254 characters
Use the PUT /Appliance/ApplianceStatus/Name API to modify this value.

HostDnsSuffix.description:
HostDnsSuffix of this appliance
When combined with Name to produce a DNS name, the DNS Name must be less than 254 characters
Use the PUT /Appliance/ApplianceStatus/HostDnsSuffix API to modify this value.

InstalledLicenses.description: Summary information for each installed product license.

IsReadOnly.description: True when this appliance is a read only mode.

ApplianceStatus.description: The current runlevel of the appliance.

LogTime.description: The date the task was created.

UserId.description: The database ID of the user that performed the activity.

FileName.description: Name of file that is transferred (Read-only).

ArchiveServerId.description: Database ID of the asset or directory to execute this task on.

ArchiveServerName.description: Name of the asset or directory name the task was executed against (Read-only).

NetworkAddress.description: Network DNS name or IP address used to connect to the machine over the network.

Log.description: Detailed log of task execution. Only populated in results from the Tasks resource (Read-only).

ArchiveActivityLog.description: Represents an archive task that may run on a remote machine.

ArchiveServerId.description: Archive server to save backup to.

ArchiveBackupParameters.description: Information needed to archive a backup.

ServiceAccountId.description: Database ID of an asset account for connecting to the asset. Cannot be specified simultaneously with ServiceAccountName.

ServiceAccountName.description: The name of an account that is not being managed for connecting to the asset. Cannot be specified simultaneously with ServiceAccountId.

EffectiveServiceAccountName.description: The name of the account for connecting to the asset whether the account is managed or not (Read-only).

ServiceAccountDomainName.description:
The domain name of the service account if the task uses a Directory
or DirectoryAccount (Read-only).

ServiceAccountDistinguishedName.description: Distinguished name of service account (required for OpenLdap).

EffectiveServiceAccountDistinguishedName.description: Effective distinguished name of service account (required for OpenLdap).

ServiceAccountPassword.description: If ServiceAccountId is not specified then the service account password may be set via this property. (write-only).

ServiceAccountHasPassword.description: Whether the service account has a password set or not (Read-only).

ServiceAccountHasSshKey.description: Whether the service account has an SSH key set or not (Read-only).

Port.description: Port used to connect to the system.

ServiceAccountAssetId.description: The database ID of the asset the service account belongs to (Read-only).

ServiceAccountAssetName.description: The name of the asset the service account belongs to (Read-only).

ServiceAccountAssetPlatformId.description: The database ID of the platform of the asset the service account belongs to (Read-only).

ServiceAccountAssetPlatformDisplayName.description: The platform display name of the asset the service account belongs to (Read-only).

ServiceAccountNetbiosName.description: The netbios name of the service account (Read-only).

CreatedOn.description: The UTC Date/Time the backup was taken.

CreatedByDisplayName.description: The source appliance user display name.

Size.description: The byte size of the backup. This may adjust based on the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V4.BackupRestore.Backup.Status.

Filename.description: Filename constructed using the appliance Id and date.

ApplianceId.description: The Appliance ID where the Backup originated.

ApplianceName.description: The Appliance Name where the Backup originated.

ApplianceMajorVersion.description: Major Version of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ApplianceMinorVersion.description: Minor Version of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ApplianceRevision.description: Revision of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ApplianceBuild.description: Build Version of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ApplianceLtsType.description: LtsType of the Backup's Source Appliance.

AppliancePlatform.description: Build Platform of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ApplianceVariant.description: Build Variant of the Backup's Source Appliance.

ArchiveServerId.description: Database ID of the archive server where this backup was last copied to.

ArchiveServerName.description: Name of the archive server where this backup was last copied to.

LastArchivedDate.description: Date this backup was last archived.

PasswordProtected.description: True if this backup is password protected.

PasswordIdentifier.description: Guid string identifier generated for the backup password.

GpgProtected.description: True if this backup is Gpg encrypted.

GpgPublicKeyName.description: The GPG Public Key Name if the backup is GPG encrypted.

GpgPublicKey.description: The GPG Public Key if the backup is GPG encrypted.

StartedOn.description: The UTC Date/Time the backup restore was started.

BackupVersion.description: The version of the backup being restored.

BackupId.description: Qualified ID of the backup being restored.

BackupApplianceId.description: ID of the appliance where backup was originated from.

BackupAppliancePlatform.description: Platform of the appliance where backup was originated from.

BackupRestoreStatus.description: Status indicator for a backup restore operation.

ArchiveServerId.description: Server to archive backup to after creation.

BackupSettingParameters.description: Parameters to govern creation of a backup.

BackupStatus.description: Status indicator for a backup operation.

Enabled.description: Whether to enable or disable the BMC configuration.

AdminPassword.description: Admin password to set when enabled. If disabled the password will be randomized.

Ipv4Address.description: The IPv4 address of the BMC network configuration.

Netmask.description: The netmask of the BMC network configuration.

DefaultGateway.description: The default gateway of the BMC network configuration.

MacAddress.description: The mac address of the BMC network configuration (Read-Only).

UncPath.description: Full UNC path to the data to be downloaded.

Username.description: The username used to connect to the CIFS share.

Password.description: The password for the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.CifsProperties.Username used to connect to the CIFS share.

CifsProperties.description:
When the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.DownloadType.Cifs DownloadType is specified, these additional parameters nned
            to be set to download the data.

DcSiteName.description: Site assigned to returned domain controller.

CldapPingInfo.description: Information returned from a DsGetDomain look-up.

Utilization.description: How much this CPU is being used (Read-only).

CpuStatus.description: Represents the status of a CPU.

DebugService.description: Represents a service that can emit debug logs.

StagedOn.description: Date the diagnostics package was staged.

StagedBy.description: The user that staged the diagnostics package.

ExecutionStarted.description: Date the diagnostics package started executing.

ExecutionEnded.description: Date the diagnostics package finished executing.

TotalMb.description: The total MB of space on this partition (Read-only).

AvailableMb.description: The available MB of space on this partition (Read-only). Note that this is not the space on the disk not yet allocated to a partition.

UsedMb.description: The total used MB of space used by pangaea.

PercentUsed.description: The percent that is being used.

SessionCacheUsedMb.description: The total used MB of space used by the session recording cache.

TotalSessionCacheMb.description: The total MB of space that can be used by the session recording cache.

SessionCachedPercentUsed.description: The percent of the session cache that is being used.

DiskStatus.description: Represents the status of a disk partition.

DnsRecordType.description: The DNS record type to lookup.

DownloadType.description: The protocol that will be used to connect to the remote location to download the data.

EventLevel.description: The level of severity of the event. Specifically for OS events.

BiosSerialNumber.description: Serial number of the running bios (Read-only).

HardwareStatus.description: Collection of information about the hardware of the appliance.

HostName.description:
Display name of the appliance. If the HostDnsSuffix is also set, then this value must be
            compatible with standard DNS naming rules.

HostDnsSuffix.description: When combined with the HostName, produces a Fully Qualified DNS name (FQDN).

InstallDate.description: Date this hotfix was applied to the appliance (Read-only).

HotFix.description: Describes an Operating System hotfix that has been applied to the appliance.

Url.description: Full URL to the data to be downloaded.

VerifyTlsCertificate.description:
If HTTPS is used, specifies whether to verify the SSL/TLS certificate from the server.
            It is recommended to upload the remote server's root CA to Safeguard as a trusted root certificate.

HttpProperties.description:
When the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.DownloadType.Http DownloadType is specified, these additional parameters need
            to be set to download the data.

IdentityProviderId.description: Database ID of the identity provider (Read-only).

IsSystemOwned.description: Whether this identity is owned by the system and cannot be deleted (Read-only).

EmailAddress.description: Email address of the Identity if applicable (Read-only).

FullDisplayName.description: Full display name of the Identity (Read-only).

Identity.description:
Represents an Identity.
The Identity is either a group or a user.

IsValid.description:
Is the license currently valid. This will be false if the license
has expired.

Expires.description:
The date that this license expires, or null
if it is perpetual.

LinkDuplex.description: Possible Link Duplex values of a network configuration.

NextScheduledMaintenance.description: The next time maintenance is scheduled to run.

LastScheduledMaintenance.description: The last time maintenance was ran.

ArchiveServerId.description: The database ID of the archive server to use after generating backup (for Backup MaintenanceType).

ArchiveServerName.description: The name of the archive server to use after generating backup (for Backup MaintenanceType) (Read-Only).

TimeZoneId.description: The timezone in which the schedule should run. Required when ScheduleType is not Never.

TimeZoneDisplayName.description: The description of the timezone in which the schedule should run (Read-only).

RepeatInterval.description:
How frequently to run. Units are based on ScheduleType.
e.g. every 2 days, every 3 months, every 4 weeks.

RepeatDayOfMonth.description: Which day of the month the schedule should run. Only valid when RepeatMonthlyScheduleType is DayOfMonth.

RepeatDaysOfWeek.description: Which days of the week the schedule should run. Only valid when ScheduleType is Weekly.

StartHour.description: The hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Hour' or 'Minute' schedule type).

StartMinute.description: The minute of the hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Minute' schedule type).

TimeOfDayIntervals.description: Configuration of intervals of time in a day where tasks can be executed.

MaintenanceSchedule.description: Represents how frequently automated maintenance will be performed.

AvailablePhysicalMb.description: Total available physical memory in megabytes (Read-only).

AvailableVirtualMb.description: Total available virtual memory in megabytes (Read-only).

MemoryStatus.description: Represents information about the memory resources of the appliance.

MonthlyScheduleType.description: The type of monthly schedule to run on.

NetworkDiagnosticInfo.description: Represents all fields available or required for Network Diagnostics.

NetworkDnsSuffixConfig.description: Represents the DNS suffixes that are used during network address resolution.

LinkSpeedMbps.description: The speed of the network link. When setting this property, 0 defaults to maximum supported speed.

DnsServers.description: List of DNS servers to use for domain name resolution.

DnsSuffix.description: DNS suffix for this network interface.

SystemOwned.description:
Indicates whether the interface is system owned
System owned interfaces cannot be removed.

VlanId.description: The Virtual LAN ID associated with this network interface.

NetworkInterface.description: Represents network interface configuration and status.

Prefix.description: The route prefix. Must be a numeric IPv4 or IPv6 address.

NextHop.description: The next hop or gateway address. Must be a numeric IPv4 or IPv6 or unspecified to indicate that the prefix is on-link.

Metric.description: Specifies the metric for the route. Lower metric values indicate more preferred routes.

NetworkStaticRoute.description: Represents an IPv4 or IPv6 static route entry.

NetworkAddress.description: Address of the network e.g., 192.168.1.0 or 2001:0db8:0123:4567:89ab:cdef:1234:5678.

NetworkPrefixLength.description: The length of the network prefix e.g., 24.

NetworkSubnet.description: Represents a network subnet in CIDR notation e.g., 192.168.1.0/24.

ApplianceId.description: Unique hardware-specific identifier of this node.

CheckDate.description: The UTC date/time of this health check.

SerialNumber.description: Hardware serial number of this appliance node.

LinkSpeedMbps.description: The speed of the network link.

NsLookupInfo.description: Additional information that can be specified when performing an NSLookup.

PrimaryServer.description: The primary NTP server. Use either a FQDN or IP address. Required to enable NTP client.

PrimaryNtpModes.description: NTP modes of operation for primary server.

SecondaryNtpModes.description: NTP modes of operation for secondary server.

SpecialPollInterval.description: How often to poll for time changes if NTP server is configured for SpecialInterval mode.

NtpClientConfig.description: Represents the NTP configuration on the appliance.

LastSync.description: The last time the date/time was synchronized with the server (Read-only).

NtpClientPeerInfo.description: Represents the current information about an NTP peer configured on the appliance.

RootDelay.description: The root delay of the client (Read-only).

RootDispersion.description: The root dispersion of the client (Read-only).

LastSync.description: The last time the date/time was synchronized with the server (Read-only).

PollInterval.description: How frequently the date/time will be polled  (Read-only).

PeerInfo.description: List of information about configured NTP peers.

NtpClientStatus.description: Represents the current status of the NTP client on the appliance.

Name.description: Official name of the OS running on the appliance (Read-only).

Version.description: Official version of the OS running on the appliance (Read-only).

ActivationStatus.description: Returns a description of the windows activation status.

ActivationDetails.description: Returns details of the windows activation.

UptimeInSeconds.description: How long (in seconds) the OS has been running (Read-only).

ServicePackVersion.description: Version of the OS service pack running on the appliance (Read-only).

HotFixes.description: OS hotfixes applied to the appliance (Read-only).

WindowsLicensePartialKey.description: Windows license partial key. Empty if not licensed (Read-only).

IsLicensed.description: True if the appliance operating system is licensed (Read-only).

UsingKms.description: True if the appliance operating system is licensed using a KMS server (Read-only).

OperatingSystem.description: Information about the operating system of the appliance.

PatchVersion.description: Specific to patching, will get translated to appliance Version after install.

ResolveAddressesToHostnames.description: corresponds to ping -a : "Resolve addresses to hostnames.".

NumberEchoRequests.description: corresponds to ping -n count: "Number of echo requests to send.".

BufferSize.description: corresponds to ping -l size: "Send buffer size.".

DontFragmentFlag.description: corresponds to ping -f : "Set Don't Fragment flag in packet (IPv4-only)".

TimeToLive.description: corresponds to ping -i TTL: "Time To Live.".

ServiceType.description: corresponds to ping -v TOS: "Type Of Service (IPv4-only)".

MillisecondTimeout.description: corresponds to ping -w timeout: "Timeout in milliseconds to wait for each reply.".

CountHops.description: corresponds to ping -r count: "Record route for count hops (IPv4-only)".

TimestampForCountHops.description: corresponds to ping -s count: "Timestamp for count hops (IPv4-only).".

PingInfo.description: Represents optional information for the Ping Network Diagnostic tool.

BackupId.description: The guid of the backup file.

BackupFilename.description: The file name of the backup file.

Name.description: An english identifier for the warning.

Message.description: A localized string Message for the condition encountered.

ProductVersion.description: Product version of the code/image of the appliance.

QueryOption.description: Query options used when performing an NS Lookup.

Uri.description: The proxy URI or host information.

UserName.description: The user name for proxy calls.

Port.description: The port setting for the proxy.

RemoteDownloadProperties.description: Information on where Safeguard should download the specified data from.

PercentComplete.description: How much of work associated with the request is complete (Read-only).

Cancellable.description: Is the request cancellable? (Read-only). The value of this flag is a suggestion only.

AcceptanceDuration.description: Duration that request was being validated for acceptance (Read-only).

StartTime.description: When was the request work started (Read-only).

TotalDuration.description: Total duration that request was active (Read-only).

Message.description: Last message associated with the request (Read-only).

RunLevel.description: Represents the runlevel of the appliance.

SafeguardServiceName.description: List of services hosted by safeguard.

EndMinute.description: Minute the interval ends. The end minute is not included in the window interval.

Iterations.description: Number of times to execute tasks during schedule window (derived using RepeatInterval for Hourly and Minute schedule types).

ScheduleInterval.description: Interval of time in which to execute tasks.

ScheduleType.description: Represents how often the specified schedule will run.

UsedMb.description: The total used MB of space used by secure files.

TotalMb.description: The total MB of space that can be used by secure files.

PercentUsed.description: The percent of available space for secure files that is being used.

SecureFilesDiskStatus.description: Represents the status of the disk space used by secure files.

NetworkDebugEnabled.description:
Whether to enable detailed network connection debug logging. Requires restarting the appliance for
all changes to take effect.

Services.description: List of service names these settings apply to.

ServiceDebug.description: Represents the configuration for how services log debug data.

MinValue.description: The setting's minimum limit value, if the value is numeric in nature. When null, the value is either non-numeric or there is no minimum limit. (Read-only).

MaxValue.description: The setting's maximum limit value, if the setting is numeric in nature. When null, the value is either non-numeric or there is no maximum limit. (Read-only).

Options.description: Valid options for this setting. Empty for many settings. (Read-only).

Setting.description: Represents a setting used to configure the appliance or application.

Value.description: One of the setting's optional values (Read-only).

Description.description: A description of this setting option (Read-only).

SyslogFacility.description: A value used to categorize the system or application that generated the syslog event.

SyslogProtocol.description: A combination of the network connection transport protocol and Syslog message format to use.

Id.description: Unique database ID of this server configuration.

Port.description: Port to connect to syslog server.

UseSslEncryption.description: Whether to encrypt data with SSL. Only valid when using TCP protocol.

UseClientCertificate.description: Whether to authenticate to syslog server with client certificate. Requires that SyslogClientCertificate is configured. Only valid when using SSL encryption.

VerifySslCertificate.description: Verify SSL certificate if using SSL. Only valid when using SSL encryption.

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

SyslogServer.description: Represents a syslog server where data and events can be sent.

SyslogTcpFraming.description:
When using Syslog over a TCP connection, both the server and client must agree on how each
            message will be deliminated, since TCP is a stream based protocol.

Fingerprint.description: The MD5 fingerprint hash of the SSH key (Read-only).

FingerprintSha256.description: The SHA256 fingerprint hash, Base64 encoded, of the SSH key. (Read-only).

SystemSshKey.description: Represents an SSH key assigned to a system.

CurrentTime.description: The current time on the appliance (Read-only).

SystemTime.description: The current time on the appliance.

TelnetInfo.description: Additional settings when attempting to establish a Telnet connection.

TargetApplianceId.description: corresponds to inverse of tracert -d : "Do not resolve addresses to hostnames.".

MbToTransfer.description: Number of MB to transfer to the target. Specify a number between 1 - 1000.

Days.description: The days component of this time interval.

Hours.description: The hours component of this time interval.

Milliseconds.description: The milliseconds component of this time interval.

Minutes.description: The minutes component of this time interval.

Seconds.description: The seconds component of this time interval.

TotalMilliseconds.description: The value of this time interval in whole and fractional milliseconds.

TimeOfDayType.description: Method to determine the time of day that scheduled tasks will run.

ResolveAddressesToHostnames.description: corresponds to inverse of tracert -d : "Do not resolve addresses to hostnames.".

MillisecondTimeout.description: corresponds to tracert -w  timeout: "Wait timeout milliseconds for each reply.".

CountHops.description: corresponds to tracert -h maximum_hops : "Maximum number of hops to search for target.".

TracerouteInfo.description: Represents optional information for the Traceroute and Ping Network Diagnostic tools.

ClientIpAddress.description: IP address of the user client that caused the change (Read-only).

UserName.description: UserName of the user that caused the change (Read-only).

DomainName.description: Domain name of the user that caused the change (Read-only).

UserDisplayName.description: DisplayName of the user that caused the change (Read-only).

UserWasGlobalAdmin.description: Was the user in the GlobalAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasDirectoryAdmin.description: Was the user in the DirectoryAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasAuditor.description: Was the user in the Auditor role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasApplicationAuditor.description: Was the user in the ApplicationAuditor role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasSystemAuditor.description: Was the user in the SystemAuditor role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasAssetAdmin.description: Was the user in the AssetAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasPartitionOwner.description: Was the user a partition owner at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasApplianceAdmin.description: Was the user in the ApplianceAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasPolicyAdmin.description: Was the user in the PolicyAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasUserAdmin.description: Was the user in the UserAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasHelpdeskAdmin.description: Was the user in the HelpdeskAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).

UserWasOperationsAdmin.description: Was the user in the OperationsAdmin role at the time of the change? (Read-only).
